column1: description

The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONFLICT feature in Bluetooth in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows remote attackers to bypass intended pairing restrictions via a crafted device, aka internal bug 26551752.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). Also, libxml2 versions <=2.9.4 are vulnerable if you are using an C++ compiler which supports the %opt flag with namespace scope and you have done neither `xmlInitParserCtxt(context)` nor `context->interceptor = interceptor`. NOTE: PHP, if you are using libxml through the PHP extension, is not affected.python-libxml: if you are using libxml through the python libxml binding, is not affected. Als

libXcursor before 1.1.15 has a buffer overflow vulnerability in Xcursor_read_cursor_dir() in Xcursor/Xcursor.c. It could be exploited to crash a program that reads cursor files. The vulnerability is triggered by a specially crafted cursor file. The cursor library does not appear to be used for any other known vulnerabilities. This issue is relevant to X.Org libXcursor distributions and does not affect any other software. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2016-5196. Users are advised to upgrade to libXcursor 1.1.15 or later. There are no known workarounds for this issue.

The asn1_write function in ssl/d1_lib.c in OpenSSL 1.0.2 before 1.0.2a does not check the return value of an object's write function, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (buffer under-read and segmentation fault) or possibly have unspecified other impact via crafted DER data. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2015-3177.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as High because it was found to be exploitable in the wild.

The BnGraphicBufferConsumer::onTransact function in libs/gui/IGraphicBufferConsumer.cpp in libstagefright in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not initialize a certain slot variable, which allows attackers to obtain sensitive information, and consequently bypass an unspecified protection mechanism, by triggering a HAL_TRANSACT_SET_WINDOW transaction, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26338109.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796.

In the function walk_string() there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an incorrect bounds check. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this issue. Account for the target app not removing trailing newline characters within a paragraph. The fix will be to limit the walk_string() function to the first paragraph of text. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android ID: Android ID: A-740786696 References: https://source.android.com/security/bulletin/pixel/2019-06-01

The LDAP server in the eCard LDAP server implementation in LDAP before 2.1.10 and 3.x before 3.0.4 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (daemon crash) via a crafted search request that triggers an incorrectly initialized `use_ptr' variable, as demonstrated by a hexadecimal 0x0000 value, related to the ldb_msg_get_string function in ldb_msg.c.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an CVE-2007-0740 issue.

In the function 'strupper_talloc_n_handle' there is a possible buffer overflow due to an integer overflow in the 'push_codepoint_handle' function. This could lead to a remote code execution with no stack trace in user mode. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability in order to arduino. Although the vector is Android, it is a real vulnerability. Product: Android. Versions: 4.4.4, 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-35269634. *NOTE: This issue is being addressed by a new Android system build; users are not currently required to update.

The smb2_setup_authenticate function in fs/cifs/smb2_authenticate.c in the Linux kernel before 4.13.15 allows local users to cause a denial of service (system crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted /proc/fs/cifs/stat file.  NOTE: PHP is required to be installed for the vulnerability to be exploitable.

The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONFLICT feature in BlueZ before 5.24 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (daemon crash) via a crafted device that responds to the discovery event of a new controller that was not paired with the default controller. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2016-7066.

In Poco JSON library 2.0.0 to 2.1.1 and 2.2.0 to 2.2.1 there's a use after free in the TextReaderImpl::_parseNumber function in JSON/src/TextReaderImpl.cpp. It allows an attacker to craft a malicious file to cause a denial of service or possibly have unspecified other impact. This is related to the issue in 2.1.0 in which an attacker can overwrite memory with a crafted file. This is related to the issue in 2.2.0 in which an attacker can overwrite memory with a crafted file.

The ssl3_get_client_hello function in ssl/ssl3_get_client_hello.c in OpenSSL 0.9.8zc, 1.0.0 before 1.0.0o, 1.0.1 before 1.0.1j, and 1.0.2 before 1.0.2b, when using DTLS, does not properly handle rehandshakes, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (invalid memory access) via a malformed DTLS packet, as demonstrated by a packet to a server that has already provided a valid DTLS handshake.  NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2014-3568.  SF-only-issue: As of 1.0.1t, DTLS1_client_hello_timeout is n

The BN_mod_exp function in OpenSSL 1.0.2 before 1.0.2a, 1.0.1t, and 1.0.0s before 1.0.0s does not properly handle zero moduli, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via unspecified vectors, related to the "mont_exp" function.  NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from CVE-2015-3900 because of different vulnerable functions.

The libxml2 library before 2.9.4 Attackers can cause a denial of service (crash) via markup that is not recognized by the lexer but is parsed by an alternative text parser.  NOTE: this issue exists because of an XML_PARSE_NOENT (ignore entities) option.  If you are using that option, the problem is not your library.

The Artifex Software, Inc. PDF reader allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (infinite loop and CPU consumption) via a crafted file that triggers a large number of iterations.  NOTE: PHP code was used in the initial advisory to cover an older version of an application that is no longer in production. This has been removed for the following refactored advisory.

The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONF_PARAMS structure in Bluetooth in Android 4.x before 4.4.4 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted device that triggers a pairing process that triggers pairing callbacks with an unexpected data structure.

In the function parse_fond in parsefond.c there is a vulnerability that allows an attacker to cause a denial of service (crash) of the application and possibly execute arbitrary code. The vulnerability is caused by an out of bounds write that occurs in the function ft_memcpy in the function create_lwfn_name in parsefond.c. The attacker can supply a long string to the function parse_fond which will cause a buffer overflow and possibly crash the application. This vulnerability affects all versions of the software.

The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONFLICT feature in BlueZ before 5.20 could be exploited to possibly cause a denial of service (system crash) of the Linux kernel due to excessive memory usage. (It was introduced in a way that did not require any pairing and did not use any pairing features.) NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incorrect check in do_pairing_conflict_check function. Upstream has assigned CVE numbers but have not assigned a specifics for this issue. Also this issue may require a long term fix to fully understand the root causes.

libcameraservice in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not require use of the CAMERA_SERVICE permission, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application that directly connects to /camera/camera2, aka internal bug 26403770. This issue is rated as Critical because it affects Android grades of Trust, aka Android Security Grade D.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as High because it was discovered during a patch production run. Product: Android. Versions: 6.0, 6.0.1, 6.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-35269632.

libvpx in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.0 before 2016-03-01 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, related to libwebm/mkvparser.cpp and other files, aka internal bug 23452792.

mediaserver in mediaserver.cpp in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not validate OMX buffer sizes, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 27207275.

The BnGraphicBufferConsumer class in libs/gui/ BnGraphicBufferConsumer.cpp in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 mishandles buffer objects, which allows attackers to obtain sensitive information, and consequently bypass an unspecified protection mechanism, via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26338116.

The libxml2 XML parser before 2.9.4 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption or DoS) via an empty document with many elements in a way that triggers many times the growth limit of the parser's element stack.  NOTE: this issue exists only in the libxml2 XML parser and does not impact the libxml2 HTML parser.

The BnGraphicBufferConsumer class in libs/gui/IGraphicBufferConsumer.cpp in Android 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not initialize a field, which allows attackers to obtain sensitive information, and consequently bypass an unspecified protection mechanism, by triggering a high CPU usage situation, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26338116. This issue is rated as Critical due to the possibility of remote denial of service. Product: Android. Versions: 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 2016-03-01. Android ID: A-34819016.

The XvQueryAdaptors function in Xv in the X11 library, before version 1.0.9a, does not check if the X server and client have the same major version, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service by using an X server with a higher major version.  NOTE: this issue exists because of an error in the upstream CVE-2009-0791 patch. The fix should be applied to the upstream X server project to fix this issue. This issue is not present in Xv 1.0.9a.

libxml2 before 2.7.6, when entity substitution is used, does not ensure that the resulting character data remains within a node, as XML parser (libxml2) before 2.7.6 does not ensure that the resulting character data remains within a node, as is demonstrated by a crafted external entity reference, aka an "external entity attack".

In the smb_setlen function in libsmbclient before 3.0.3-5 and 3.0.4-2, an integer overflow allows a remote attacker to cause a denial of service or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted client message. This is related to the length field in the SMB header, which is not properly restricted. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2010-0765.

In PHP before 5.4.40, 5.5.x before 5.5.21, and 5.6.x before 5.6.4, the Phar extension allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted filename, related to the phar_analyze_path() function in ext/phar/phar.c, a different vulnerability than CVE-2015-5158. It triggers when the Phar::__construct() function calls phar_analyze_path() with a relative filename that begins with /..\/.  NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2015-5158.

In PHP before 5.4.40, 5.5.x before 5.5.24, and 5.6.x before 5.6.8, the Phar extension allows a file to be created with an invalid pathname, then the file may be created in the current directory or the wrong directory. This is related to the Phar_File_Entry `createListing` function, which is supposed to only create entries for files in the specified directory. If `$entry->getPath()` returns a string starting with `/`, it should not be used as the `$path` in `Phar_File_Entry->extract($path)`. NOTE: PHP native code is not available in this snippet.

The g_signal_emit function in GLib before 2.36.0 has a type confusion issue in the g_signal_accumulate_mismatches function in gsignal.c. The function attempts to match a mismatched argument with a vfunc argument by comparing the GType of the mismatched argument with the GType of each vfunc argument. If the GTypes are the same but are not equal, GLib incorrectly coerces the GType to a compatible GType and then compares the arguments again. As a result, GLib incorrectly determines which vfunc to use and may use a vfunc with a wrong first argument. This can be used to construct a remote code execution exploit. This issue has been fixed in 2.36.0. This has been found in the following functions: g_signal_accumulate_mismatches, g_signal_call_mismatched

The g_quark_from_static_string function in GLib before 2.36.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (use-after-free and application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted string, related to an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-5919.

In the function my_object_increment_retval_error of the file source/my-object.c, there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an integer overflow. This may lead to information disclosure over the secure heap with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability in order to affect code execution. The min security label of the process is 'untrusted'. This is a priority 2 vulnerability.

The my_object_increment_val function in libmyobject.so in the program allows local users to cause a denial of service (object corruption) by leveraging failure to properly initialize an object, as demonstrated by a val member, which is not initialized.

The g_ascii_strtoull function in libglib-2.0.so.0 in GnuTLS before 2.12.10 allows local users to bypass intended access restrictions and read arbitrary files by leveraging the CAP_MAC_ADMIN capability to change the permissions of a file to that of a regular file, and then using the eaccess function to check the access permission of a file.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). - https://www.cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2015-5750

In the js_parse_args function in js.c there is a possible out of bounds write due to an incorrect bounds check. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-783548552 References: https://source.android.com/security/bulletin/2019-08-01

The OpenSSL implementation of TLS_PSK_DHE_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA cipher suite has a Bleichenbacher oracle vulnerability. The vulnerability exists due to a missing check for a negative validity period in the Bleichenbacher_check_cert_to_CA function in crypto/ossl_typ.c. An attacker, who is able to trigger the vulnerability, may potentially forge a new certificate that will be accepted by the peer and thus may be able to read the traffic encrypted by the peer. The vulnerability is exploitable with a peer using the TLS_PSK_DHE_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA cipher suite. Versions that are affected include 1.0.1h through 1.0.1p and 1.0.2a. Fixed in OpenSSL

The OpenSSL implementation of TLS_PSK_DHE_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA cipher suite uses a 16-byte initial value (0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00) for the sequence number (SN) field in the Encrypted Pre-Shared Key (EPK) message, which makes it easier for remote attackers to obtain sensitive information by sniffing the network. This vulnerability is due to lack of randomness in the initial value selection. One solution is to use

In the BN_mod_exp_mont function in crypto/bn/bn_mod.c, there is a possible out of bounds write due to an incorrect size check. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability. Product: OpenSSL. Versions: 1.0.2h-1.0.2o and 1.0.1t-1.0.1u. Android: fixed in 1.0.2p-1.0.2r and 1.0.1s-1.0.1u.

The OpenSSL 1.0.2 before 1.0.2a function ssl_parse_serverhello_tlsext in ssl/s3_srvr.c does not properly verify the 'size' of the ServerHello extension, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) via crafted TLS data.  NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from CVE-2015-3972 because the CVE-2015-3972 fix did not apply to the OpenSSL 1.0.1 branch.  Also, this issue is related to the product, not necessarily to OpenSSL.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This ORNL-1641570 issue is triggered when a user touches a button outside of a dialog and then touches the dialog. The dialog is unresponsive. Application-permissions are not a cause for a crash. Product: Android. Versions: 6.0, 6.0.1, 6.1.1. Android ID: A-32177777. References: N/A.

In the eval_condition function in exim 4.83 (and possibly other versions as well) a stack-based buffer overflow occurs when a specially crafted name is passed in. This is related to the read_name function, which is supposed to read a name from a string and then pass that name to the chop_match function. The problem is that the size of the name that can be read is not checked against the size of the buffer. This could be exploited to execute arbitrary code with the privileges of the process running exim. This has been fixed in Exim 4.84.

In the expand_string_integer function, there is a use of the strtoll function without proper length check which allows an attacker to cause a denial of service (crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted string. The vulnerability is caused by the lack of a length check in the strtoll function. This can be fixed by adding a length check to the strtoll function.

The ssl_scan_clienthello_custom_tlsext function in ssl_lib.c in OpenSSL 1.0.1 before 1.0.1i allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read and application crash) via a malformed ClientHello message.

The OpenSSL 1.0.2 before 1.0.2a function ssl_parse_serverhello_tlsext in ssl/sess.c does not properly verify the 'size' value of a ServerHello extension, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (abort) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a malformed DTLS packet, related to ssl/s_server.c. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2015-3152.

The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONFLICT feature in Bluetooth in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows remote attackers to bypass intended pairing restrictions via a crafted device, aka internal bug 26551752.

The BN_mod_exp_mont function in crypto/bn/bn_mod.c in OpenSSL 1.0.2 before 1.0.2f does not properly manage intermediate BN contexts, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via a crafted RSA operation.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incorrect free of X during the renegotiation process.  Fixed in 1.0.2f. It is also described in more detail in the advisory below.

The XpmCreateDataFromXpmImage function in libXpm.a in file libXpm/xpmread.c in Fedora 18 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (heap-based buffer over-read and application crash) via a crafted XPM file.

A denial of service vulnerability in libstagefright in Mediaserver could enable an attacker to use a specially crafted file to cause a device hang or reboot. This issue is rated as Moderate because it requires an uncommon device configuration. Product: Android. Versions: 4.4.4, 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-35763994. NOTE: this issue also exists in the stable release of Android 6.0.1.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not properly handle the presentation of multiple requests for permissions, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26677744.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796.

The PORCHE_PAIRING_PROCESSING_MULTIPLY_F24 function in crypto/pairing/aes_gcm.c in OpenSSL 1.0.2 before 1.0.2k, 1.0.1t before 1.0.1t, and 1.0.0t before 1.0.0t, when the botan command-line tool is used, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) via a .botan file that contains a PORCHE_PAIRING_PROCESSING_MULTIPLY_F24 command with insufficient operand arguments.  NOTE: this issue is not in OpenSSL 1.0.1 or 1.0.0, which have different handling of the PORCHE_PAIRING_PROCESSIN

The PDF implementation in MuPDF before 1.10 does not validate PDF Object Stream lengths, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (abort) via a crafted file, related to "denial of service vulnerability", one of several vectors described in Oliver Kellerman's progress report for the MuPDF project.

In PHP before 5.4.40, 5.5.x before 5.5.24, and 5.6.x before 5.6.8, the Xdebug extension allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption and segmentation fault) via an exception thrown during Zend AST constructor execution.

In PHP before 5.4.40, 5.5.x before 5.5.21, and 5.6.x before 5.6.4, the SPL circular reference handler can lead to a segmentation fault when handling references to unserializeable objects via unserialize() in Zend/zend_serialize.c. This is related to how circular references are handled in Zend/zend_serialize.c and Zend/zend_objects.c. This is fixed in the 5.4.40, 5.5.21 and 5.6.4 releases. VDB-23879 describes a similar issue in the context of YAML unserialization. NOTE: PHP 7 does not have this issue. Additional research has shown that the problem has been fixed in the 5.4.40, 5.5.

libcameraservice in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not require user authentication for the camera service, which allows attackers in the same process as the camera driver to gain privileges via a crafted application that uses JNI, might allow attackers to execute arbitrary code in the context of the a camera driver with no additional execution privileges to invoke the JNI function CameraService.connectCameraProcessor with a string argument that supplies the path to a camera driver, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26265403.

libcameraservice in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not require user authentication for the camera service, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application that directly calls a function to add a camera device, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26771466.

The BN_mod_exp_mont function in crypto/bn/bn_mod.c in OpenSSL 1.0.2 before 1.0.2e allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) via a zero modulus.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incorrect fix for CVE-2015-7565.  It is not related to BN_mod_exp_mont.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not ensure that a permissions-dialog is not created after a dialog for allowing other applications access to the camera has been closed, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka internal bug 26771852.

The PORCHE_PAIRING_PRODUCT function in crypto/pairing/p256_p256/s256_amx.c in OpenSSL 3.0.0 before 3.0.0-rc1.10.1 does not verify that the x and y coordinates of the curve point are valid, which might allow attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via a crafted pairing operation. This is related to a different issue than CVE-2020-19676.  NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2020-19676. Fixed in 3.0.0-rc1.10.2.

The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONFLICT feature in Bluetooth in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows remote attackers to bypass intended pairing restrictions via a crafted device, aka internal bug 26551752.

The Bochs Guest Edition (GUEST_INIT) before 2.6.7, when running on the Xen platform, does not prevent other Xen guests from mounting the CD-ROM, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted Xen configuration, as demonstrated by obtaining Dom0 LAVA secret_key privileges by booting a crafted Xen domain from an atta.c.l.net CD-ROM.

libvpx in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.0 before 2016-03-01 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, related to libwebm/mkvparser.cpp and other files, aka internal bug 23452792.

The IceAuthHandshake_parseClientHello function in auth.c in Pulse Secure (PS) Pulse 4.x before 4.1 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and daemon crash) via an unexpected server_hello message.

In the function jbig2_word_stream_buf_get_next_word(Jbig2WordStream *self, int offset, uint32_t *word) there is a buffer over-read vulnerability due to an incomplete fix for an under-read vulnerability in the function jbig2_word_stream_get_next_word of jbig2.c. The vulnerability exists in libjbig2 of Git commit 989711a and earlier. The under-read can occur when reading a word from a JBIG2 image. The under-read can cause the stream to be left in an inconsistent state. The result of the under-read will be written into the output parameter 'word'. If the under-read does not consume any bytes from the stream, then the stream will be left in an inconsistent state where the next read will attempt to consume bytes from the stream that were not there. Th

libcameraservice is a system service providing the Camera2 API for accessing the camera hardware. It is used by camera-related applications to access the camera hardware and its features. In the processCameraFrame function, there is a possible out of bounds read due to an incorrect bounds check. This could lead to remote information disclosure over Bluetooth with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is not needed for exploitation. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android ID: A-1118039251 References: https://source.android.com/security/bulletin/2018-04-01 Android ID: A-37896371

In the jbig2_decode_mmr_init function, a heap-based buffer over-read vulnerability can occur because of the use of size as the loop limit. A value of size can be larger than 4, leading to an out-of-bounds read. This will lead to denial of service.

In the function jbig2_end_of_stripe, there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an integer overflow. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability without requiring successful authentication. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-740786699 References: https://source.android.com/security/bulletin/2019-03-01
The prctl_set_vma_anon_name function in kernel/sys.c in the Linux kernel before 3.4.54 allows local users to cause a denial of service (OOPS) by writing a long string.

In the function above, a memory-related issue exists that can be triggered by a specially crafted application. More specifically, a NULL pointer dereference exists in the pte_offset_map_lock function. The issue exists because the pte_offset_map_lock function does not check if the pmd_none function returns true before trying to lock the page table spinlock. This can crash the kernel with a NULL pointer dereference when processing a specially crafted application. Exploitation of this issue requires human interaction. Common platforms are not exposed. This issue is rated as High because it directly leads to code execution. This issue is included in the CVE-2017-6074 request for a full-disk encryption module.

In the Linux kernel before 5.1.2, a double free issue was found in drivers/nvme/target/fc.c. When the fc_destroy function fails to allocate a new fc_queue, it uses a fc_queue from a pool that was not properly initialized. An attacker can use this flaw to crash the system or possibly execute arbitrary code. (This issue only occurs if the kernel is compiled with support for IO_URING; see the upstream Linux kernel commit for more information.) NOTE: this issue does not affect the FreeBSD or amd64 platforms.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). This may allow an attacker to load and process a local file, access an HTTP or FTP server, or access a file, HTTP, or FTP server to which the libxml2 process has access. This occurs because libxml2 performs a check_fileptr before doing any processing in the xmlParseFile handler, and this check is not otherwise exported. The sgi_timer_set function in kernel/time/timer.c in the Linux kernel before 2.6.39.1 does not properly handle the timer_list member, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference) by leveraging the CAP_SYS_TIME capability to change the timer parameters for another user. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). This fixes the default parser by default, which was the default behavior of libxml2 up to and including version 2.9.4. If you need to retain the previous behavior of libxml2 (for backward compatibility), you can define the symbol XML_PARSE_NOENT before you include the libxml header.

The mishandling of format specifiers in the sprintf function in the Android media framework before 5.1.1 and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, an internal bug number and additional research are needed for completing this description, such as context-dependent attack vectors, aka internal bug 26413177.

In the perf_output_begin function, there is a flaw in the way memory is allocated for the output buffer. A buffer underflow can occur in the event of a wraparound. This may result in an excessive amount of data being read from the buffer. This may expose sensitive information in the memory.

The TLS 1.3 implementation in OpenSSL 1.0.2 before 1.0.2a allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (heap-based buffer over-read and application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via an unexpected record type in a crafted ClientHello message, related to ciphersuites/t1_lib.c, record/t1_enc.c, and record/t1_lib.c.  NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-5705.  It is recommended to upgrade to the fixed OpenSSL 1.0.2a or higher.  There are no wordings for details on the latter.

The libxml2 XML parser before 2.9.4 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption or memory consumption) via an empty document with many elements. The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-03 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as High because it was found to be vulnerable in a few unique ways. This is a product of the new way that window managers pass events to their children. NOTE: this issue exists because it is common for Android developers to overlook checking SurfaceControl permission bits when creating a window, because they file the request in WindowManager.LayoutParams even after they have been processed by a SurfaceComposer.

In the function vmci_transport_dgram_dequeue in vmci_transport.c there is a possible out-of-bound read due to a missing bounds check on skb->data. This could lead to reading of uninitialized or unexpected data. First encountered in Android 7.0 and 7.1.1 (depends on UAPI) 76d2-da3f-a13d 38d2-da3f-a13d 392c-da3f-a13d 3941-da3f-a13d 3966-da3f-a13d 3987-da3f-a13d 39a2-da3f-a13d 39ba-da3f-a13d 39d5-da3f-

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI 1.x before 1.5.2 allows remote attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and possibly other unspecified impacts via a series of crafted windows, as demonstrated by clipping and rendering errors, aka internal bug 26596101.
In the hash_str function, there is a possible integer overflow with large inputs. This is due to the fact that the shift operation is unchecked and the value of "hash" can overflow.

libcameraservice in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not require use of the ICameraService::dump method, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application that directly dumps files, leading to boot loop vulnerability, and then executing arbitrary code, aka internal bug 26265403.

The SOAP client in PHP before 5.10.0 does not properly implement the zeroconf behavior, which allows remote SOAP servers to conduct SOAP requests on behalf of the client, and to perform XML external entity (XXE) attacks. NOTE: PHP 5.10.0 is not yet available.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. Additionally, the dialog might be missing in some cases, as the window manager may choose to use a different dialog for the error case, aka internal bug 26677797.

The IGDstartelt function in libcurl before 7.31.4 and 7.40.0-rc1-N1034 before 7.40.0-rc2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) via an isoTransport: request in which the path has one more segment than the IGDstartelt function expects.

The BnGraphicBufferConsumer::onTransact function in libs/gui/IGraphicBufferConsumer.cpp in libcutils in Android 5.x before 5.x before 5.0.9 and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not initialize a certain slot variable, which allows attackers to obtain sensitive information, and consequently bypass an unspecified protection mechanism, by triggering an ATTACH_BUFFER action, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26338116. SP-7311122: "IGraphicBufferConsumer::onTransact" lacks initialisation of a slot variable.
The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI 1.x before 1.5.2 allows remote attackers to conduct tap-jacking attacks via clickable areas in overlays to a app.

The lzxd_decompress function in mspack.c in BusyBox before 1.27.2 has an integer underflow that allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted LZX compressed file.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2010-4152.  NOTE: the crash is more likely to occur with small input.  NOTE: this issue is different from CVE-2010-4152.  NOTE: the crash is more likely to occur with small input.

An information disclosure vulnerability in the Android media framework (libstagefright). Product: Android. Versions: 4.4.4, 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 8.0. Android ID: A-63522430. References: Q3 2017.

The mod_dumpio module in the Apache HTTP Server 1.3.x before 1.3.21 and 2.x before 2.0.6 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted DumpIO request.  NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from CVE-2007-4564 because in that previous assignment, it was found that a different, unknown vulnerability had been present.

The BnGraphicBufferConsumer::onTransact function in libs/gui/IGraphicBufferConsumer.cpp in libcutils in Android 5.x before 5.x before 5.0.9 and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not initialize a certain slot variable, which allows attackers to obtain sensitive information, and consequently bypass an unspecified protection mechanism, by triggering an ATTACH_BUFFER action, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26338109.

In the xfs_attr3_leaf_add function in fs/xfs/xfs_aops.c in the XFS filesystem before 3.1.5, there is an integer overflow that allows physically proximate attackers to cause a denial of service (system crash) via a crafted extended attribute write operation that results in a large file size.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2013-4312.  NOTE: this issue is related to the xfs_attr3_leaf_add function, not xfs_attr3_leaf_find.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists in the kernel, but does not affect userspace.

In the function btrfs_search_slot in fs/btrfs/btrfs_alloc.c there is a possible out of bounds write due to a missing bounds check. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed for exploitation. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android ID: A-783548552 References: https://source.android.com/security/bulletin/2018-04-01 Android Bug: https://bugs.chromium.org/p/android/issues/detail?id=7907759

In libxml2 before 2.9.4, an XML external entity (XXE) definition that has an XML parameter entity (PE) reference as its content, as entity, and an unparsed entity, has been allowed. This allows an XML entity inclusion vulnerability, where an attacker could use an entity in an XML document to load and parse content from an external source (such as a file, a DNS zone, etc.) within the confines of the libxml2 library. The issue is that libxml2 does not verify the content of an unparsed entity against the entity substitution setting, which allows an attacker to bypass an xml_reader_set_substitute_entities() restriction. The issue is fixed by limiting the scope of the unparsed entity content to the entity content itself. This has no effect on the entity and its PE reference. This occurs because the content model for an unparsed entity in the document type definition ha

In the function 'magic_getparam' in libmagic.so, there are four switch cases for the parameter 'param'. If the value of 'param' is not in the range of 0 to 3, the function will set errno to EINVAL and return -1. This is a buffer overflow vulnerability because the function does not check the size of the 'val' parameter. This could lead to a stack buffer overflow with a very small stack size. This issue is rated as High because it could be used to launch a denial of service attack.

The GNU tar 1.26 through 1.32 and 1.34 and 1.35 through 1.38, as used in GNU/Linux distributions and other environments, allows attackers to cause a denial of service (divide-by-zero error and daemon process termination) via a crafted archive, a different vulnerability than CVE-2017-5180. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-5180. Patches are available from GNU but it is possible that updates will not incorporate these patches.

The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONFLICT feature in Bluetooth in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows remote attackers to bypass intended pairing restrictions via a crafted device, aka internal bug 26551752.

In the function do_remount_sb() there is a possible out-of-bound read due to an incorrect bounds check. This could lead to remote information disclosure over Bluetooth with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is not needed for exploitation. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-1118039251 References: https://source.android.com/security/bulletin/2019-02-01

In the krb5_gss_inquire_context function in libkrb5.a there is a possible out-of-bound read due to an incorrect calculations with krb5_timestamp. This could lead to remote information disclosure over Bluetooth with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is not needed for exploitation. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-110216176 More info: https://source.android.com/security/bulletin/2019-02-01

In the function gss_unseal of gssapi.c in GnuTLS 0.6.10, an attacker is given more data than expected, leading to an out-of-bounds write on the heap. This could be exploited to run code as a result of an attacker-supplied data. (Additional details follow in a following update.) This issue is rated as High because it could be used to launch a fully-authenticated attack against an administrator of a system. All versions of GnuTLS up to 0.6.10 are affected. The maximum allowed size for the output buffer is calculated based on the size of the input buffer, which is missing in the gss_unseal function. This has been fixed in GnuTLS 0.6.11. It is recommended that you upgrade to a newer version of GnuTLS. There are no workarounds for thi

In the gss_pseudo_random function in gss_clnt/gss_pseudorand.c in libkadm5 before 1.13.2, the length of the output is not checked to not exceed the size of the output buffer, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (stack memory corruption) via a length field in a crafted GSS-API response.

The prctl_set_vma_anon_name function in kernel/sys.c in Linux before 3.4.5 does not prevent access to a vma pointer, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel memory or cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) via a crafted string-to-vma call.

The fcntl function in libc before 2.23, when called with the F_DUPFD or F_DUPFD_CLOEXEC function arguments, allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (open file descriptor leak) via a large number.  NOTE: this issue exists because of an integer overflow in the fcntl_dupfd function in NeDi/libc.  It is a similar issue to CVE-2017-5168.

libcameraservice in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not require use of the ICameraService::dump method, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application that directly dumps, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26265403.
In the Linux kernel before 5.1.2, a double free issue was found in drivers/nvme/target/fc.c.

In the function h2_stream_add_header in mod_http2.c there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an integer overflow. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability in order to ensure the intended software state is not modified. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-740786692 References: https://source.android.com/security/bulletin/pixel/2019-06-01

The BnGraphicBufferConsumer::onTransact function in libs/gui/IGraphicBufferConsumer.cpp in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not initialize a certain slot variable, which allows attackers to obtain sensitive information, and consequently bypass an unspecified protection mechanism, by reading a crafted media file, aka internal bug 26338112.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). This affects xmlParseFile, xmlParseMemory, xmlParseIO in libxml2 versions 2.7.6 and earlier, and 2.7.7 and later when using an external entity loader (i.e., when the caller specified ENT_SUCCESSION_ALLOWED). Also see xmlParseFile, xmlParseMemory, xmlParseIO in libxslt 1.1.28 and earlier, and 1.1.29 and later whe

The SASL PLAIN authentication mechanism in IRC server in use does not properly handle client messages, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (service abort) via a crafted AUTHENTICATE message, as demonstrated by a message that does not include the expected "authzid".  NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2013-4346.

The mbedtls_aes_setkey function in AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) in mbed TLS before 2.7.0 and 2.14.0 allows local guest users to cause a denial of service (memory corruption) by leveraging unspecified factors.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not ensure that the Android ID of a preexisting account is not also present in the auth.accounts array, which allows attackers to conduct device-wide account hijacking attacks via an application that targets the Android ID, as demonstrated by an application that targets the Google account, aka internal bug 26677792.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796.

The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONFLICT feature in Bluetooth in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows remote attackers to bypass intended pairing restrictions via a crafted device, aka internal bug 26551752.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796.

In the gdImageCreate function in the GD Graphics Library (aka libgd) before 2.2.4, an integer overflow allows attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a large sx value.  NOTE: this issue exists only in 2.2.x and 2.1.x releases. Other versions are not affected.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI 1.x before 1.5.2 allows remote attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and possibly other unspecified impacts via a crafted application that overlays a dialog on top of a window, aka internal bug 26677796. This vulnerability is due to missing support for the OVER_SCAN_CONTAIN flag. An attack requires the attacker to place the dialog window on top of another to "steal" clicks from the underlying application. This issue is rated as High because it allows an remote part of the application to gain access to a user's actions, as demonstrated by cliking on a different application's buttons, as demonstrated by cliking on a different application's buttons, which leads to the disclosure of confidential application-stored data. Product: Android. Versions: 1.x before 1.5.

The BnGraphicBufferConsumer::onTransact function in libs/gui/IGraphicBufferConsumer.cpp in libcutils in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 mishandles contexts, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26877992. SP-OS73547735-13573730.

The TFTP (Trivial File Transfer Protocol) server in tftpd in Red Hat Enterprise Linux 6 before 6.2, Scientific Linux 6.2 before 6.2, and 6.2 before 6.3 mishandles file system ownership and permissions, which allows local users to gain privileges via a (1) write operation to a world-writable file or (2) a strip command on a file with a setuid or setgid bit set, as demonstrated by a file ownership change from root:root to user:sambashare or from root:root to user:shadow.  NOTE: PHP is a separate product.

The rpcap_hdr structure in libpcap, as used in the pcap_open_offline function in libpcap, mishandles certain pcap files with large file sizes, which results in a denial of service (memory consumption) by allocating more memory than is necessary. This affects pcap_file_magic_check in file pcap/pcap.c, pcap_open_offline in file pcap/api.c, and pcap_read_header in file pcap/read.c in libpcap 1.9.0 and 1.8.0-precise1. An attacker can provide a crafted pcap file to launch a denial of service attack. It is also exploitable by launching a denial of service attack with a crafted pcap file. This vulnerability appears to have been fixed in after upgrading to libpcap 1.9.

In the accept function, the ntohs function is used to ensure that the port number is in host byte order before it is used, which means that if the port number is zero, then the port number is assumed to be in network byte order and will wrap around to a non-zero value, resulting in an integer underflow that may allow an attacker to cause a denial of service. This has been fixed in Git 1.7.10 and later. Also, prior to 1.7.10, if the port number is zero, then the port number is assumed to be in host byte order and will wrap around to a non-zero value. We recommend updating to Git 1.7.10 or later if you can, or applying the following patch if you cannot:

In PHP before 5.4.40, 5.5.x before 5.5.24, and 5.6.x before 5.6.8, the Xdebug extension allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted filename in a function call to the file() function. NOTE: this issue is user-triggered.

In the function get_icu_value_src_php, the parameter 'tag_name' is passed to the function get_icu_value_internal without any sanitization. This may lead to a buffer overflow if 'tag_name' contains a specially crafted string. This issue is similar to CVE-2017-5833.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-05 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. User interaction is not needed for an attacker to exploit this vulnerability. Product: Android. Versions: 6.0, 6.0.1, 6.1, 6.1.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-36636007. NOTE: this issue is described as an implementation detail of the permission-dialog and its UI, not a fundamental framework bug.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). This occurs because entity types are not restricted to be owned by the document they are being used by. The impact of this failure is the possibility of obtaining libxml2-parser-compatible XML data as input, along with a libxml2-parser, and making this parser accept the XML data as its input, with the same libxml2-parser as its DTD (or XSD) resolver. This may be used in an attack against the libxml2-parser to expose software that uses th

Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). This may also allow an attack者 to cause a denial of service in libxml2 before libxml2 is used for a document-source-specific vector. This flaw is fixed in libxml2 2.9.4, 2.8.0-2.8.7 inclusive, and 2.7.8-2.7.12 inclusive. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). It is recommended in all cases to disable the default loader and request loader usage, and set the entity loader from the loader context if the caller requested them. Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). The Linux kernel before 3.3.2, when KVM is used in guest mode and VMXNET3 is initialized, allows guest OS commands to be executed on the host via a hypervisor crafted data file and crafted network packet, related to "TNG-44615" and "TNG-44616".

The ras_server_thread in hw/scsi/vmw_vscsi_ras.c in QEMU before 1.7.2 has an Integer Overflow that allows local guest OS administrators to cause a denial of service (guest OS crash) via a large number of hosts in a vSphere Client search. This is due to the limit parameter in the process_request function being set to a value from the max_requests variable, which can be changed by the user. NOTE: The problem exists in the hw/scsi/vmw_vscsi_ras.c file.

In the function finish_object of git-builtin-upload-archive.c, there is a possible NULL pointer dereference due to an incorrect check. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-740786692 References: https://source.android.com/security/bulletin/pixel/2019-03-01

In the function traverse_commit_list in git-rev-list.c there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an integer overflow. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability in order to bring about denial of service. The min version of the Android platform required to exploit this vulnerability is Android 4.4.4. The Android version displaying the vulnerability is 5.0.1. Vulnerability is decribed in details in the advisory. You can view the vulnerability details at the following link: https://www.exploit-db.com/exploits/40487/

In the function ecryptfs_privileged_open, there is a possible out-of-order execution due to an incorrect order of operations. This could lead to local flags being changed and then ignored. This is problem is limited to build variants that include cmd_crypt and enable ECRYPTFS_ENCRYPT_ON_WRITE. Product: Android. Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android ID: A-111603051. References: CVE-2016-10865.

In the function proc_fill_super() there is a vulnerability that can be exploited by a malicious user-mode program. A out-of-bound memory access will occur because the size of the buffer is not properly checked in the function proc_read_data() while handling the /proc filesystem. This may lead to information disclosure, denial of service or possibly code execution. This issue is rated as High because it is possible to crash the system or execute arbitrary code. The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONFLICT_IDLE_TIMEOUT feature in Bluetooth in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows remote attackers to bypass intended pairing restrictions via a crafted device, aka internal bug 26551752.

The DH_check_pub_key function in crypto/dh/dh_check.c in OpenSSL 1.0.2 before 1.0.2f does not ensure that DH_check_p_gen check has been performed, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) via an arbitrary server DH packet.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI 1.x before 1.x 2012-09-14 allows remote attackers to conduct tap-jacking attacks via an on-screen message located in a container that follows the intended target container, as demonstrated by a clickable container of a target container, aka internal bug 27950931.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. Additionally, implementing a patch for a different vulnerability discovered in 2016 which affects a different component, this bug cannot be combined with the previous one. This bug does not affect Android TV versions of these releases.

In the NeXTDecode function, there is a buffer overflow vulnerability because there is an inappropriate overflow check in the code. By crafting data that is passed to the function, an attacker may be able to bypass this check and overwrite memory. This vulnerability affects the function NeXTDecode in libtiff 3.8.0 and earlier. The impact of the vulnerability can be used for executing arbitrary code under the context of the process. This vulnerability is diffcult to exploit due to the check in place.

mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-06-01 does not validate OMX buffer sizes, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 27207275.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016 might allow attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as High because it was found to be vulnerabl

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as High because it was discovered during a UI permission-dialog implementation. Product: Android. Versions: 4.0-4.4.4, 5.0.0-5.0.2, 5.1.0-5.1.1, 6.0, 2016-04-01. Android ID: A-34827340

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796.

The jbd2_journal_start_commit function in fs/jbd2/journal.c in the JBD2 (aka Journaling Buffer Design) package before 2.0.3 does not properly handle out-of-sequence commit requests, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (JBD2 committer process crash) via unspecified user-input, aka “local users can cause a JBD2 process to crash.” This is related to the “uid” issue.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CID bf14474440.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-05 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as High because it was discovered during a UI permission-dialog implementation. Product: Android. Versions: 6.0, 6.0.1, 6.0.2, 6.1, 6.1.1, 6.1.2, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-35763183.

In the do_anonymous_page function in mm/memory_mgmt.c in the Linux kernel before 3.11.7, there is an incorrect handling of memory pages, which allows physically proximate memory access by unprivileged userspace. This is related to mm/memory_mgmt.c, a bug has been introduced in mm/hugetlb.c, a bug has been introduced in fs/aio.c, and a bug has been introduced in fs/pipe.c. It can be exploited to cause a denial of service (system crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code. This issue is rated as High because it involves a combination of new aspects within the kernel. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2012-2669. Many of the specific issues are not complete.

The mishandling of a specially crafted TP_CONTEXT_KERNEL_SP_CHANGE notification in the Linux kernel before 3.19.4, when KVM is used, allows local users to cause a denial of service (guest crash) via a crafted application that leverages an incorrect guest sp change notification. In the function above, there is a use-after-free issue in the 'umount_tree' function. The issue lies in the 'change_mnt_propagation' function. The 'change_mnt_propagation' function does not check if the 'mnt_parent' pointer is NULL before calling 'change_mnt_propagation' recursively. This can lead to a segmentation fault when 'mnt_parent' is NULL. The 'change_mnt_propagation' function is called in the 'umount_tree' function after the 'umount_mnt' function is called. The 'umount_mnt' function sets the 'mnt_parent' pointer to NULL in the 'mnt' structure. The 'change_mnt_propagation' function should check if the 'mnt_parent' pointer is NULL before calling itself recursively.

The ssl3_get_client_hello function in ssl3_clnt.c in OpenSSL 1.0.1 before 1.0.1i allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and daemon crash) via an unexpected NewSessionTicket message, which triggers client-side session resumption.  NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from the previous description. The SSLv3 "client hello" handling in the client code in OpenSSL 0.9.8zf, 1.0.0o, and 1.0.1 might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a malformed Confidentiality Encryption request, as demonstrated by a message with an invalid cipher suite.  NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from the previous description. The client code in OpenSSL 0.9.8z

The ssl3_get_client_hello function in ssl/ssl3_get_client_hello.c in OpenSSL 1.0.1 before 1.0.1i allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and daemon crash) by starting a TLS session without a client hello message, related to a "fatal error in OpenSSL 1.0.1" and "possible error in OpenSSL 1.0.1."

In the sspi_SecureHandleGetLowerPointer function, a negative value of handle->dwLower is cast to a size_t type and then bitwise NOT (~) operator is applied to it. The result of this operation is then assigned to the pointer. This can lead to undefined behavior if handle->dwLower is a large positive number because the resulting value will be a large negative number. This can be exploited to read memory locations that are not accessible due to address space layout randomization (ASLR) in a non-x86 platform.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as High because it was discovered during an interactive attempt and because it directly and positively exploits the user's training of awareness.

In the function adjust_scalar_min_max_vals of bpf_verifier.c, there is a possible out of bounds write due to an incorrect bounds check. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-783548552 References: https://source.android.com/security/bulletin/2019-01-01

The krb5_kdf_extract function in krb5/kdf_util.c in libkrb5 in MIT Kerberos 5 (aka krb5) before 1.13.2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and daemon crash) via a crafted ticket.

In the function above, there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an integer overflow. This could lead to remote code execution with no saved state. The problematic code is the xfrm_user_rcv_msg function in net/xfrm/xfrm_user.c. The impacted code is: if (type == (XFRM_MSG_GETSA - XFRM_MSG_BASE) || type == (XFRM_MSG_GETPOLICY - XFRM_MSG_BASE)) && (nlh->nlmsg_flags & NLM_F_DUMP) { struct netlink_dump_control c = { .dump = link->dump, .done = link->done, }; return netlink_dump_start(net->xfrm.nlsk, skb, nlh, &c);

The usb_parse_configuration function in drivers/usb/core/config.c in the Linux kernel before 3.16.2 does not properly handle descriptors for multiple interfaces, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (integer overflow and memory corruption) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted USB device driver.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an CVE-2012-0570 issue.  NOTE: this issue is not in the usb_parse_configuration function in drivers/usb/core/config.c in the Linux kernel before 3.16.2.

The node_t data structure lacks a field for storing the number of children a node has. This can lead to bugs in the code that relies on this information. The flawed code does not check if the node has a right child before deciding whether to add a 1 or 0 to the bit stream. An attacker can send a specially crafted input to cause an out-of-bounds read in the add_bit() function.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). Also, libxml2 versions <=2.9.4 are vulnerable if you are using an C library that is built to use libxml2 for its XML parsing capabilities. NOTE: libxml2 through 2.9.0 is also vulnerable to a different, unrelated, and different exploit. This issue is named by default: with the starter kit.

The zip_stream_read function in zip.c in the ZIP file format support in the OpenJDK implementation of Java allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (infinite loop and daemon thread leak) via a crafted ZIP file that triggers a large number of iteration in the zip_stream_read function.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI up to 5.0.2 allows remote attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks via a crafted application that uses (1) Android SystemWebViews, (2) Android 4.4 WebViews, or (3) other applications to present a partially overlapping window of a web-page to the user, which includes interactions from the user and interactions from the attacker, aka internal bug 26677796. The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI up to 5.0.2 allows remote attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks by laying out a dialog on top of an app within a certain mouse-event sequence, aka internal bug 26677796. In the function parse_ip_address_ex, there is a use of the function atoi() without checking if the input string is a valid integer. This may lead to an integer overflow if the input string is crafted specially. This is problematic because the atoi() function in PHP is implemented with a signed integer overflow check, which will return a different result compared to the C standard library's atoi() function. The vulnerability exists in the PHP 5.4.x and 5.5.x series. This issue is patched in PHP 5.5.30, 5.4.45, and 5.6.7. Users of these versions are advised to upgrade. Other versions are unaffected.

The libxml2 XML parser before 2.7.6, as used in Google Chrome before 13.0.782.215, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service or possibly have unspecified other impact via an invalid encoding in an XML header.

The MAPI_Attr structure in MAPI (Message Application Program Interface) contains an 'unsigned char *data' field. If this data field is allocated and then the MAPI_Attr structure is deallocated, accessing the 'data' field after the MAPI_Attr structure is deallocated will lead to a use-after-free vulnerability. This could be exploited to access sensitive information or possibly execute code. This issue is similar to CVE-2005-3981.

The YACC parser generator (yacc or bison) is vulnerable to a stack-based denial of service (DoS) attack if it is used with the `--graph` option, as demonstrated by a yacc-based attack in a DoS report from 2010. This occurs because the `--graph` option builds a static call graph for the parse rules, which consumes a substantial amount of memory. An attacker can provide a specially crafted input file to cause a denial of service. This version of yacc and bison has patched this issue. It is recommended to limit the size of the input file or use the `--max-loop-iterations` option to prevent a denial of service.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI has a possible out-of-bounds write. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is not needed for exploitation. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-740786738. NOTE: This issue is related to the permission-dialog, which is a system dialog. The vulnerability could be impacting other UI-dialogs.

The user_match function in the Linux kernel's subsystem for key management (crypto/keys/process_2.c) uses a flawed approach to match a key with user-provided description. A NULL pointer dereference in the key_payload_reserve function (lib/key.c) could lead to denial of service. This is due to the key_payload_reserve function failing to verify the provided key payload size. A remote authenticated attacker could use this flaw to crash the system.

libcameraservice is a userspace service for interacting with the Android camera HAL. This service has several security issues, most of which have been addressed in Android 6.0 (Marshmallow). In libexif, there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an integer overflow. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed for exploitation. Product: Android. Versions: 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-35269632. NOTE: This issue only occurs for devices that use the libion version of libutils.

NOTE: this issue is due to a one-time use of a medium with a unique value, which may be a result of an earlier use.

libtiff 4.0.7 has a buffer over-read in `libtiff/tif_unpack.c::read_tile' in the `strip' path. Remote attackers may cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted file that triggers a large unpack value in the unnamed `tile' struct. This is related to an integer overflow issue in `libtiff/tif_unpack.c::unpack_data'. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-18073.

NOTE: this issue is more for code coverage than for fixing actual data.

libpng 1.2.5 has a heap-based buffer over-read vulnerability in png_handle_IHDR in png.c. This may lead to remote denial of service. It is possible to trigger the vulnerability minipng.c:mingetdecoder by sending a crafted PNG image.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI 2.x before 2.x allows remote attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks by laying out a crafted application window atop a non-crafted window, aka internal bug 26677796.
The XML parser in Python before 3.4.1 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption and denial of service) via an extremely large number of attributes in an XML node.

In the jas_matrix_create function, there is a heap based buffer overflow vulnerability because the jas_alloc2 function is used without any error checks. A quantity of memory larger than expected is allocated, and then written to without any check. When this memory is read, it results in reading uninitialized memory, which may result in sensitive information disclosure. This attack appears to be exploitable by causing an application to crash or possibly execute arbitrary code. This vulnerability could be exploited in a Denial of Service attack-application crash, as well as executing arbitrary code.
The parse_string function in sf_disp.c in libsndfile before 1.0.28 and 1.0.27 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (assertion failure and application exit) via a crafted FLAC file.
