The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONFLICT feature in Bluetooth in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows remote attackers to bypass intended pairing restrictions via a crafted device, aka internal bug 26551752.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). This fixes an XML external entity (XXE) issue in the parser. Please note that libxml2 must be configured with the --with-http support in order for HTTP access to work. Users of Debian-derived distributions should note that in their main libxml2 package they also need to install the libxml2-openssl package to build with OpenSSL support. Users of Ubuntu should install the libxml2-openssl1.0 package instead. Fixed in libxml2 2

The ras_getcmap function in ras_dec.c in mediaserver in Android 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, aka internal bug 26070016.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not properly handle near-full-screen dialogs, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, environment, or input, via a summary message, to launch the dialog with high confidence, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26677792.

The xml_parse_into_struct function in ext/xml/xml.c in PHP before 5.5.35, 5.6.x before 5.6.21, and 7.x before 7.0.6 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (buffer under-read and segmentation fault) or possibly have unspecified other impact via crafted XML data in the second argument, leading to a parser level of zero.

The TLSv1_client_hello_cb in OpenSSL 1.0.x before 1.0.1  and 1.0.2  before 1.0.2-11 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and daemon crash) via an arbitrary byte in the ClientHello message, which triggers an invalid extension.

The lha_decode_uint32 function in liblha 2.0 before alpha4 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (divide-by-zero error) via a malformed LHA archive.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an INCOMPLETE code example. A complete exploit would require further research into the lha_decode_uint16 function.

The LHA_write_header function in archive_write_set_format_7zip in libarchive 3.3.3 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (infinite loop and CPU consumption) via a crafted 7z archive that has a large number of files. NOTE: this issue exists because of an intellectual property rights issue; 7-Zip is a registered trademark of Igor Pavlov.

In ImageMagick before 7.0.8-50, coders/mpeg2.c in the MPEG2 decoder allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted media file. This is related to the MPEG2 decoder and may be linked to the "black_frame" variable in mpeg2.c. It may provide the keys to exploit this in some unknown way.

In the _nss_mymachines_getgrnam_r function, there is a buffer over-read issue in the "gr->gr_mem = (char**) buffer;" line due to the absence of a length check in the "sd_bus_message_read" function. This could lead to a crash of the application if the reply is smaller than expected. User-controlled input is not used in this function, but the function is vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack by sending a specially crafted message. The severity of this issue is Medium (6.5).

In the _nss_mymachines_getpwnam_r function, a memory-buffer overflow vulnerability in the sd_bus_message_read function can occur due to missing bounds checking. A remote attacker could use this flaw to crash a program by sending a specially crafted message. This issue is rated as High due to the possibility of remote code execution. This attack is unique to libdbus. The good news is that this bug is not exposed to the system's DBUS_SESSION_BUS environment variable.

In the given C code snippet, the vulnerability is in the line where the unlink() function is called. The unlink() function is used to remove a file from the file system. If the file is removed before the free() function is called, then the memory allocated with malloc() will not be freed, leading to a memory leak. This can result in a Denial of Service (DoS) condition.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4, 5.0 before 5.0.2, 5.1 before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796.

In the Linux kernel before 4.14, there is a use-after-free issue in the ext4_fill_super function (fs/ext4/super.c) related to reading the superblock. A negative value is returned in the event of an error, causing the superblock cache to be left in an inconsistent state. This may be used to facilitate recovery attacks. This issue has been fixed in Linux kernel 4.14 and later. NOTE: this issue only occurs if the kernel is configured with support for 64bit platforms.

In smp_process_keypress_notification of smp_act.cc, there is a possible out of bounds read due to an incorrect bounds check. An elevation of privilege vulnerability in libstagefright in Mediaserver could enable a local malicious application to execute arbitrary code within the context of a privileged process. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). This has been found in at least two cases in the wild. The problem has been fixed in Git commit 91f2d615191d5e1d2d238817e0384f20d9ecf1cb. Security fixes.

In the function `nntp_fetch_headers` there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to a missing bounds check. This could lead to remote code execution with no saved state in the current process. If the variable `last` is greater than the value of `first`, there is a race condition. This will also lead to memory corruption. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability. The following versions are affected: Android 4.x, 5.0, 5.1, 6.x, 7.0, 7.1, 7.1.1, 8.0, 8.1, and 9.0. Android TV 4.x, 5.0, 6.0, 7.0, 7.1, 7.1.1, and 8.0. Android Wear 3.x. Firmware versions of Googl

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). This fixes the default parser flags case from XML entity inclusion during DTD validation and external DTD subset loading. The problem also occurs with XML data with an external entity that is a URL and a local file. The URL will be reachable and the local file content will be inserted if the user agent is configured that way. The sh_op function in libr/anal/p/anal.c in radare2 1.2.1 processes data read from an input file. It mismanages the order of bytes in a 16-bit value, which could be exploited by a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code via a crafted file. It is critical that this function not only correctly identifies the operation type but also correctly interprets the operand.
A vulnerability in the Android media framework (n/a). Product: Android. Versions: 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 8.0. Android ID: A-63581671.

The libxml2 XML parser before 2.9.4 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption or DoS) via an empty document with many elements in the XML data, a different vulnerability than CVE-2014-8143.

In the function `ParseRiffHeaderConfig` there is a vulnerability in the line `if (ds64_chunk.sampleCount64 < total_samples)`. The condition will be true if `ds64_chunk.sampleCount64` is less than `total_samples`, which means `total_samples` is greater than `ds64_chunk.sampleCount64`. However, `total_samples` is calculated from `infilesize`, which is the size of the file, and `ds64_chunk.sampleCount64` is the number of samples in the file. If the file size is larger than the number of samples, `total_samples` will be greater than `ds64_chunk.sampleCount64`, causing the condition to be true. The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796.

In the _single_string_alloc_and_copy function, a heap buffer overflow vulnerability exists due to the lack of a maximum limit on the length of the string that can be copied. This can be exploited by sending a specially crafted string as an argument to the _single_string_alloc_and_copy function. A successful exploit could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code within the context of the application. This issue is rated as High due to the possibility of persistent damage and scope.

The parse_args function in archive_read_support_filter_lzop.c in libarchive before 3.2.2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read and application crash) via a crafted .cpio archive. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2011-4103.

In the function `pixHtmlViewer`, there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an integer overflow. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. The gnuplot_parse_parm function in gnuplot_parse.c in Gnuplot before 4.6 does not initialize a certain integer variable, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted term command.

The Framework Composition API in Android 6.x before 2016-04-18 allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka internal bug 26405520. Apps are not directly responsible for the security of their activities, so the highest-priority user-level permissions should be requested for the least possible set of permissions that allow an attacker to gain the highest-possible-privileges for an activity to which the app has been bound. This issue is fixed in Android 6.0.1 and 7.0 and 7.1.1, and 8.0, and 9.0, and does not affect Android 5.1.1.

DBus-GLib 0.73 disregards the access flag of exported GObject properties, which allows local users to bypass intended access restrictions and possibly cause a denial of service by modifying properties, as demonstrated by the "access-flags" property in (1) software/org.gtk.gtk3example.gobject.xml and (2) software/org.gtk.gtk3example.gobject2.xml in Gtk3 example applications. This vulnerability is similar to CVE-2014-10061. Although the DBus-GLib functionality is used by many applications, the issue is specific to GObject properties. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2014-10061.

The nf_conntrack_helper_reply function in net/netfilter/nf_conntrack_helper.c in the Linux kernel before 3.2.24 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (use-after-free and system crash) via a malformed DCCP packet, as demonstrated by a packet that lacks a destination port.

The decoder in libstagefright in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not properly consider the bits per pixel when allocating internal buffers, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26323456.

The Crypto API in the Linux kernel before 3.2.3 does not limit the number of keys that can be generated for a single key type, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (system hang) via a crafted application that generates keys in a loop. In PHP before 5.4.40, 5.5.x before 5.5.24, and 5.6.x before 5.6.8, the Spl class classes (Spl.php, SplArray.php, SplAtomic.php, SplEnum.php, SplFixedArray.php, SplHeap.php, SplObjectStorage.php, SplPriorityQueue.php, SplQueue.php, SplStack.php, SplDoublyLinkedList.php, and SplMaxHeap.php) use a specially crafted YAML file to instantiate objects, and the Spl class itself does not use the __toString method, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (execution and memory consumption) via a crafted YAML file.

In PHP before 5.4.40, 5.5.x before 5.5.24, and 5.6.x before 5.6.8, the interpreter needs to allocate memory for SPL objects in the main scope when calling a function with Scope & This scope, but it does not allocate enough memory, omitting the last element of the internal SplDoublyLinkedList array, which leaks an SplDoublyLinkedList object with one element. This triggers a call to a user-supplied function (provided as the second argument to spl_ptr_functions) to handle the leak. The user function can then, for example, append the leaked object to a global array. This could be used to implement a type of attack that exploits the fact that PHP memory management is not completely synchronized with that of C. The mptsas_process_scsi_io_request function in mptsas.c in the Linux kernel before 3.1 does not properly initialize a certain status variable, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel memory via a crafted SCSI request.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI until 1.x before 1.7.7, 2.x before 2.0.4, and 2.1.x before 2.1.2 allows remote attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks by laying out a crafted overlay window on top of an adjacent window for reading of the user's Android UI without any permissions. NOTE: although the name of this vulnerability might suggest a need for users to explicitly acknowledge or "opt-into" a security attack, in reality, attackers can arrange for an overlay to be displayed in any location of the screen within the application where the user is already focused when the overlay is to be shown, which is a form of "accessibility services" attack. This issue is related to and a result of the Android permission model.

In libsndfile before 1.0.28, an error in the *header_read()* function (common.c) and associated *header_parse()* function (header.c) means a crafted W64 file can cause a stack-based buffer overflow. The culprit is that *header_read()* does not check for a sane value for *num_chan* (the number of channels). Instead, it expects *num_chan* to be between 1 and 512, but it does not check that *num_chan* * *sf_format_check* is <= 65536. Consequently, if *num_chan* and *sf_format_check* are chosen in such a way that *num_chan* * *sf_format_check* is greater than 65536, a stack buffer overflow occurs. This is patched in 1.

In the *GdImagePtr GdImage::createFromPng ( string $filename )* function, there is a reachable assertion failure due to an unreachable code segment. This assertion failure occurs in the *gd_jpeg_decode_ex* function. The assertion *( jpeg->interchange_format == FALSE )* is reached in the *gd_jpeg_decode_ex* function, which should never be reached because the *gd_jpeg_decode* function is used instead. This causes an assertion failure in the *gd_jpeg_decode_ex* function. The issue is that the *gd_jpeg_decode* function does not set the *jpeg->interchange_format* variable to *FALSE*. In libsndfile before 1.0.28, an integer overflow allows a remote code execution exploit involving a crafted Ogg SNDFILE opcode packet. The bug is in the function sf_vorbis_parse_identification() in sf_vorbis.c. The culprit is a line in the while loop that reads "if (sf_vorbis_get_byte (ps)) break;". If this line is removed, the exploit does not work. The fix is to replace the line with "if ((sf_vorbis_get_byte (ps) != 0x7F) || (sf_vorbis_get_32 (ps) != (sf_count_t)(-1)))" to prevent an integer overflow.

In the function serdes_probe of the file serdes.c there is a memory leak issue because of the function devm_kzalloc. The allocated memory is not freed when an error occurs. This can result in a memory leak or even a denial of service. The allocated memory is large enough to hold a struct serdes_ctrl. The unconditional return in the error case of devm_kzalloc without a call to devm_kfree leads to a memory leak. The devm_kzalloc function returns a pointer to the allocated memory on success and an error code on failure. If an error occurs, the memory should be freed before returning.

In the function above, there are five conditions to check before returning true. If any of these conditions are met, the function should return false. However, the function does not have a return statement for the case where none of the conditions are met. This could lead to undefined behavior. The function should return a default value at the end of the function to ensure that all possible paths return a value.

In the function http_rxchunk in http.c there is a vulnerability in the way strtoul is used. strtoul is used to convert a hexadecimal number in the received buffer to an unsigned long integer. If the function does not recognize a valid hexadecimal number, it will return zero. The problem is that the variable q is not checked to see if it points to the end of the string. As a result, if q points to the beginning of the string, strtoul will return zero, and the program will crash. This issue is similar to CVE-2011-3374.

The code snippet is about parsing IP packets in C. It first checks the IP version and then parses the IP header based on the version. If the version is 4, it checks the length of the IP packet and the length of the IP header. If the length of the IP packet is less than the length of the IP header, it returns a result indicating that the packet is not IP. If the length of the IP header is greater than or equal to the length of the IP packet, it returns a result indicating that the packet is too short. If the version is 6, it does the same thing but for IPv6 packets. The code also checks the next header of the IP packet and processes the header based on the next header.

In the function DetachBlob, there is a use-after-free vulnerability because the function does not zero out the blob_info structure before returning. A malicious blob_info structure can be passed to an OpenSSL function and will be used for subsequent OpenSSL function calls. This could allow an attacker to possibly crash the OpenSSL library, execute code, or possibly cause a denial of service. This issue is rated as High due to the possibility of significant damage.

The mishmash_parse_header function in mishmash.c has a buffer over-read vulnerability in the gd2_read_header function. This could lead to remote information disclosure over the internet in circumstances where a image file is uploaded. This has been fixed in upstream and will be included in the 0.6.21 release.

In the function gup_huge_pmd, there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an incorrect bounds check for the pages array. This could lead to remote code execution with no saved state with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-78354855 multiple_safes_from_one_unsafe Line: 78354855

mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-06-01 does not validate OMX buffer sizes, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 27207275.

The gdImageFillToBorder function in the GD library 2.0.37 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (segmentation fault and application crash) via a negative foreground color value.  NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from CVE-2011-11049 because it had a different vulnerability.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is fixed in 2016-06-01 and 2016-06-03. NOTE: this issue is NOT exposed to the public in Android 6.0 (Marshmallow).

The Framework_adb_auth_client_get_auth_token function in hw/auth/Framework.c in mbed TLS before 2.7.12 does not properly initialize some variables, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory corruption) or possibly have unspecified other impact via an error response.

The TreeNodesRemoved function in TreeView.cpp in Kirigami before 5.1.0 allows attackers to cause a denial of service by opening a document containing a large number of image nodes. This is related to the performLoadChildren function in loadjob.cpp.

The prctl_set_vma_anon_name function in kernel/sys.c in Android before 5.1.1 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (vma object name hash collision and daemon crash) via a crafted application name. This triggers an assertion failure. Product: Android. Versions: 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 8.0. Android ID: A-35267045.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI until_l_r1 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks by positioning a partially transparent dialog on top of a button in unauthorized parts of a user's screen.  This may compromise the user's ability to interact with that button and possibly other buttons, as demonstrated by being able to unintentionally tap the dialog's ack button. This attack is named for the user interface concept of "tappingjacked" or "tapjacking" to describe the attack. Product: Android. Versions: 4.2.2, 4.3.1, 4.4.4, 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-352

The strncpy POSIX function (defined in the string.h header) copies not more than n characters from the location pointed to by src to dst. If the length of the string located at src is less than n, strncpy will pad the remaining characters with zeros ('\0') until n characters in total have been written. This can lead to a buffer overflow if the string located at src is longer than n.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI 2.x before r163 and 3.x before r34 allows remote attackers to conduct harassing or divulging activities via a crafted application that triggers a UI permission-dialog. It is also possible that attackers may use this vulnerability to invoke the permission-dialog on behalf of another application, thus posing a different vulnerability.  NOTE: this issue exists because the permission-dialog code is also used by the Settings application.  NOTE: this issue is fixed in "r34" and "2.1.10.1 and 3.1.10.1 for 2.x and 3.x respectively.",

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Framework UI has an optimization for redundant permissions that can be bypassed because it checks AppOpsService for permission to read the device identifier before checking if the app requests the permission. This allows an attacker in a privileged app to determine if a normal app requests the permission and writes zero to the corresponding entry in the local device owner keys file. This may expose a higher-risk app's device owner to additional risks. Product: Android. Versions: 4.4.4, 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-34749322.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4, 5.0 before 5.0.2, 5.1 before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not properly handle runtime permissions, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, environment, or location. Successful attack vectors include using an application to access sensitive environment information, which can be used to further an attacker's objective. This CVE ID is unique from CVE-2016-0402, CVE-2016-0403, CVE-2016-0404, CVE-2016-0405, and CVE-2016-0406. This issue is rated as Score: High. Versions Affec

The ssl3_get_client_hello function in ssl/ssl3_get_client_hello.c in OpenSSL before 0.9.8zc, 1.0.0 before 1.0.0r, and 1.0.1 before 1.0.1m does not properly parse the Server Name extension (clientHello.server_name), which allows man-in-the-middle attackers to cause a denial of service (server crash) by sniffing the network, as demonstrated by a packet capture in which the Server Name extension is not the first data element in the ClientHello message, aka the "server name extension vulnerability" or SSL_VULNERABILITY.

A2DP (Advanced Audio Distribution Profile) is a profile for the AVRCP (Advanced Video Remote Control Profile) specification that allows for the streaming of audio data between a Bluetooth-enabled device (the remote controller) and a device that supports audio input over Bluetooth (the audio input device). The A2DP profile defines the media interface and the associated protocols for carrying audio data between a Bluetooth device and an audio application. The media interface consists of an audio data interface and an audio configuration interface. The audio data interface carries the audio data and the audio configuration interface carries the audio settings. The audio data interface is a data interface that carries PCM (Pulse Code Modulation) audio data. The audio configuration interface is a control interface that carries audio settings. The audio data interface is a data interface that carries PCM (Pulse Code Modulation) audio data. The audio configuration interface is a control interface that carries audio settings.

The ssl3_get_client_hello function in ssl/s3_clnt.c in OpenSSL 1.0.1 before 1.0.1i allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and daemon crash) via an unexpected new_session_ticket message, as demonstrated by a message from an OpenSSH (1.2.2a) 6.79.0a client.

The Bluetooth HCI command packet (in the form of a write_hci_command function) in libs/bluez/hci.c in BlueZ before 5.28 does not properly verify the length of HCI command packets, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (system crash) via a crafted packet.

The H.264 decoder in Mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not initialize a certain slice parameter, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, aka internal bug 28195620.

In the btif_dm_pin_reply function of bluetooth stack in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01, an integer overflow can occur if the PIN code is abnormally long. This can cause an incorrect PIN check to be performed in the BTA_DmPinReply function. This is due to the passkey calculation not being limited by a maximum value. Product: Android. Versions: 4.0.0-4.4.4, 5.0.0-5.0.2, 5.1.0-5.1.1, 6.0.0-2016-04-01. Android ID: A

The media server in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not validate OMX buffer sizes, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 27207275.

In the impeg2d_dec_seq_ext_data function in decoder.c there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an integer overflow. This could lead to remote code execution with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed to exploit this vulnerability. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-783548552 References: Upstream kernel

The libxml2 library before 2.9.4 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via an empty document with many elements in the XML Procedure: Loading an XML document with many elements in a loop.

The readU16 function in the C code snippet above can be vulnerable to a buffer overflow error if the offset value is greater than the size of the data array. This is because the C language does not perform array bound checking and if the offset value is greater than the size of the data array, it will access memory beyond the allocated space, potentially corrupting data and/or causing execution to crash.

In the code snippet provided, there is a vulnerability in the line of code where the left shift operator is used. The left shift operator is used to shift the bits of the number to the left by the specified number of positions. In this case, the number of positions is 8. This means that the most significant byte of the number is shifted out and the leftmost bit is shifted into the least significant byte of the number. This can result in a loss of information if the number is negative.

The FLACResampleInit in resampler.c in libFLAC in FFmpeg before 2.0.2 does not properly check for integer overflows, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted STREAMINFO block.

In the code snippet provided, there is a vulnerability in the line *dst++ = src[1][i] << 8;. The value of the variable i is not checked to ensure it is within the bounds of the array. If i is larger than the size of the array, this will result in undefined behavior. This can lead to a buffer overflow if the array is used in a context that expects a smaller size.
The libxml2 library before 2.9.4 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption or crash) via an empty document with DTD.

A remote code execution vulnerability in libavc in Mediaserver could enable an attacker using a specially crafted file to cause memory corruption during media file and data processing. This issue is rated as Critical due to the possibility of remote code execution within the context of the Mediaserver process. Product: Android. Versions: 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-34097672.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-05 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796.

The impeg2d_bit_stream_init function in libstagefright in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.x before 2016-03-01 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, aka internal bug 26076806.

In the ixheaacd_shiftrountine_with_rnd_hq function, there is a vulnerability in the shift operation. The shift value is not checked for whether it is within the valid range. A value of 32 will cause an arithmetic overflow in the ixheaacd_shl32_sat function, leading to undefined behavior. The shift values must be in the range [0,31].

The H.264 decoder in IVIFAE_OMX_DEC_Video_Decode_Index in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not check for the correct number of bytes in the bitstream, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption and data corruption) via a crafted media file, aka internal bug 26961620.

The libmpeg2 MPEG4ES_HEADER_SIZE in mpeg4video.c in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.0 before 2016-02-01 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, aka internal bug 26071620.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with system replacements, if the following conditions are met: a) a libxml2 software library is used in the recipient's application; b) the application enables XML external entity (XXE) loading; c) the application uses default XML user settings; d) the application sends XML data to a third-party server that is owned by the vulnerability author; e) the application does not limit the potential for XML attacks in the application's code. The vulnerability is due to faulty XML user settings. NOTE: the vendor suggests upgrading to a version 2.9.2 or later, if available. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2007-5692. NOTE: the issue is not present in libxml2 version 2.7.6.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-05 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as High because it was found to be exploitable in the wild.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). This occurs because libxml2 performs a check_file() lookup for the entity/DTD and the validation of the DTD/schema occurs in the context of the existing document (and not in a restricted directory). To exploit this failure, user must open a file within a restricted directory, and the contents of a file accessible to the user (usually located in a different restricted directory) must be included in an entity or DTD in the file. The vpx_free function in libwebm in WebM in GPAC before 0.7.1 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted free call.  NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2011-1767.  The memory management code is not the issue; the problem is that the free function is not being used to deallocate all the memory associated with a chunk of data that was allocated with malloc in the same thread.  This is a result of the application incorrectly handling exception handling in a way that would lead to multiple concurrently-active jmp_buf variables.

The avrc_pars_browsing_cmd_rsp function in avrc/avrc_pars_browsing_cmd.c in AVRCP (Advanced Audio Distribution Rendering Protocol) Library in BlueZ before 5.28 and 6.x before 6.10 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a Browsing_Get_Element_Attr response that has a length field set to 0xFF. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incorrect calculation in the Browsing_Get_Element_Attr response handling in avrc_pars_browsing_cmd.c; a 0xFF length value should be rejected.

The vp9_fht16x16_c function in libwebm in mediaserver in Chrome before 13.0.782.215 on Windows does not properly handle errors, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted mkv file.

The avrc_pars_browsing_cmd_rsp function in avrc/avrc_pars_tg.cc in Liberald Blader's AvrcParserTest in Android 5.0.x before 5.1.1 LMY49G and 6.0.x before 2016-02-01 does not properly handle Browsing State information, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted Browsing_State_List response, aka internal bug 26361682.

The gnutls_session_get_data function in lib/gnutls_session.c in libgnutls before 2.10.11 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and crash) via a malformed Certificate Verify message.

The avrc_pars_browsing_cmd function in avrc/avrc_pars_browsing_cmd.c in Liberald Blader's Avrc Parser (avrc_pars) before 1.4.1 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a Browsing command with a long name component in an SDP record, which triggers a heap-based buffer over-read. The vp9_fht8x8_c function in libwebm in mediaserver in Chrome before 13.0.782.215 on Windows does not properly handle errors, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted mkv file.

In the function CheckSad, the variable max_sad is passed as an unsigned int. The function ReferenceSAD and SAD are expected to return an unsigned int value. The function ReferenceSAD calculates the reference value for exp_sad and the function SAD calculates the value of exp_sad. If the value of reference_sad is less than or equal to max_sad, then it asserts that exp_sad is equal to reference_sad. If the value of reference_sad is greater than max_sad, then it asserts that exp_sad is greater than or equal to reference_sad.

The ImageMagick 7.0.8-50 Q16 OpenCL compiler does not properly scope the memory objects, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via a crafted OpenCL kernel, related to OpenCL-6131K-build.c.

A race condition in the implementation of the Paint function in the SkBitmap class in Skia before 2016-03-25 allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive information, and consequently bypass an unspecified of CORS, via a crafted application, related to Bitmap_device.

Google Test, included in this package, uses a type-based dispatching mechanism to select the right TestCase factory function (Google Test, p. 104). This can result in a downcast from a void* to a derived class pointer, leading to undefined behavior if the object is sliced (Google Test, p. 105). This issue is only relevant if the object is sliced. If the object is not sliced, the downcast will be safe. This issue is relevant for all compilers and platforms, including GCC 4.8 and 4.9. The issue is fixed in Google Test 1.7.0 and later. A workaround is to not use void* for factory functions.

The Framework_CMD_JPEG_SetResolution function in base/core/jpeg/framework_jpeg.cpp in libstagefright in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26365346.

The avrc_pars_browsing_cmd function in avrc/avrc_pars_tg.c in Liberald Blader's Avrctl before 0.3.10-11 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a Browsing command with crafted data, which triggers a heap-based buffer overflow.

The FLACExtractor class in libstagefright in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49G, and 6.x before 2016-02-01 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read and memory corruption) via a crafted media file, aka internal bug 26366256.
